module_map.responsibility: File metadata collection and representation. Defines Meta struct and extracts all filesystem properties (permissions, dates, owner, size, symlinks, git status, etc.) via Meta::from_path().

Command-line flags and block configuration. Defines display blocks (Permission, User, Group, Size, Date, Name, INode, Links, GitStatus, Context, etc.), layout options (Grid/Tree), sorting rules, color/icon/date formatting flags.
submodules: blocks.rs (604L) - Block enum and block configuration
sorting.rs (553L) - Sort order and sorter definitions
icons.rs (362L) - Icon theme and when-to-show flags
size.rs (159L) - Size unit and format flags
hyperlink.rs (171L) - Symlink arrow and hyperlink modes

module_map.responsibility: Theme management for colors and icons. Resolves semantic colors (e.g., directory, executable, error) and icon definitions based on file type and name.
submodules: color.rs (529L) - Color theme with semantic mappings
icon.rs (868L) - Icon theme with extension and name-based lookup

module_map.responsibility: Terminal output formatting. Renders Meta objects to aligned grid or tree layout with colors, icons, hyperlinks. Core: grid(), tree(), get_output() (Block-by-Block rendering). get_output() orchestrates Block rendering using meta properties and block type.
Main orchestration: fetch() collects Meta objects, sort() orders them, display() renders output. Ties together Meta collection, sorting, and display rendering.
Sorting implementations. Defines comparator functions for SortColumn enum (Name, Size, Date, Extension, Version, etc.). Called by core.sort() to order metas before display.
ANSI color parsing and rendering. Elem enum (semantic color categories), Ansi struct for color representation.
Git repository status tracking. GitCache wraps git2 for file status (modified, staged, untracked, etc.).
Icon lookup and Unicode rendering. Icons struct wraps theme icons, provides by_name() and by_extension() lookups.
YAML config file parsing (colors, icons, blocks, sorting, date format, etc.). Merges CLI flags with file-based configuration.

data_flow.key_function: main() -> parse_args() via clap; config_file.rs loads YAML config
Meta::from_path(path, dereference, permission_flag, ...) recursively via core.rs::fetch()
types_produced: Meta struct with: name, path, permissions, date, owner, file_type, size, symlink, indicator, inode, links, git_status, access_control

data_flow.key_function: core.sort() calls display::sort() with SortColumn comparators from flags.sorting
Iterate flags.blocks.0 (Vec<Block>), match each Block enum, call meta.field.render(colors, flags, ...)
types_produced: Vec<String> (one string per block: Permission|Size|Date|Name etc., with colors/icons applied)

data_flow.key_function: grid() calls inner_display_grid() -> term_grid::Grid; tree() calls inner_display_tree()
types_produced: Grid (term_grid crate) with Cell[][], Column[], formatting rules
Tree structure with indentation and edges

data_flow.key_function: Grid.render() -> String; tree() assembles formatted strings

cross_module_hubs.reason: Hub for rendering. Imports: flags (Block, Flags, Layout), meta (Meta), color (Colors), icon (Icons), git_theme (GitTheme), sort (sort logic). Called by core.rs::display(). Coordinates all rendering logic: fetches from meta, applies colors/icons, builds grid/tree.

Hub for data collection. Imports 14 submodules (name, size, date, owner, permissions, filetype, symlink, inode, links, access_control, git_file_status, windows_*). Called by core.rs::fetch() and by display.rs. Meta::from_path() is the primary data collection entry point; aggregate of all filesystem metadata extraction.

Main orchestrator. Imports: meta, display, flags, color, git, icon, sort. Called by main.rs. Coordinates fetch -> sort -> display pipeline. Bridges configuration (Flags) with data (Meta) and output rendering.

change_proposal.title: Add SHA-256 Checksum Display Column
files_to_modify: src/flags/blocks.rs - Add 'Checksum' to Block enum (after Links, before GitStatus)
src/meta/mod.rs - Add 'checksum: Option<Checksum>' field to Meta struct
src/meta/checksum.rs (new file) - Create Checksum struct with sha256 computation and render() method
src/display.rs - Add Block::Checksum arm in get_output() match block (~line 375-378)
new_types: Checksum struct with fields: digest: String (hex). Implement render(colors: &Colors) -> String method.
Add Block::Checksum enum variant with get_header() returning 'SHA-256' (or 'Checksum').
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow the Size/Date/Owner pattern: (1) Add Block enum variant in flags/blocks.rs. (2) Create new type in meta/FIELD.rs (e.g., meta/checksum.rs) with render() method matching signature: fn render(&self, colors: &Colors) -> String. (3) Add Option<Checksum> to Meta struct. (4) Populate in Meta::from_path() by calling Checksum::from_path(path, dereference). (5) In display.rs::get_output(), add match arm: Block::Checksum => block_vec.push(match &meta.checksum { Some(cs) => cs.render(colors), None => colorize_missing("?") }),
change_proposal.integration_point: Block system is the integration point. Checksum field becomes optional; render logic reuses existing pattern in display.rs. Checksum computation happens once during Meta::from_path() (similar to Size calculation). Color lookup via Elem enum (add Elem::Checksum semantic color).
risks: Performance: SHA-256 I/O on every file walk is expensive. Mitigate: (a) Add --blocks checksum flag to make it opt-in. (b) Cache checksum by inode/mtime. (c) Only compute for regular files (skip dirs/symlinks).
Windows compatibility: Ensure Checksum::from_path() handles both Unix and Windows paths. See meta/windows_utils.rs pattern for cross-platform file reading.
Sorting: If user wants to sort by checksum, requires new SortColumn::Checksum in sort.rs. Duplicate detection use case may want this.
Memory: Large file checksums not a concern; String digest is ~64 bytes. But storing in every Meta adds 64 bytes per file in memory.
Symlink handling: Currently Meta respects dereference flag. Checksum must decide: follow symlinks or report error? Consistency: match Size behavior (compute on target file if dereference=true).

tool_usage.purpose: Get module structure and line/function counts for all 52 files
Extract Meta struct definition, from_path() signature, submodule imports (14 imports)
Extract grid(), tree(), get_output() signatures and display module imports
Extract Core struct, fetch/sort/display methods, and core.rs imports
Trace from_path() call chain: 15 callers, 22 callees; shows data flow from metadata collection
Trace grid() call chain: 3 callers (display, main, run), 16 callees (get_output, inner_display_grid); shows display rendering pipeline
Extract Block enum definition, get_header() implementation, 13 imports
Verify Meta struct field types and count (15 fields)
Extract Block enum variants and get_header() mapping to display strings
Extract get_output() function showing Block match arms and render() pattern per block type

summary.architecture_pattern: Layered pipeline: Input (Flags + Config) -> Metadata Collection (meta/) -> Sorting (sort.rs) -> Theme Resolution (theme/) -> Display Rendering (display.rs) -> Output. Core.rs orchestrates the pipeline.
summary.key_abstraction: Block enum + render pattern. Each Block variant maps to a Meta field and render method. Adding new column = (1) Block enum variant, (2) Meta field, (3) render() method, (4) get_output() match arm.
summary.dependency_flow: core.rs calls meta.from_path() -> display.grid/tree() -> display.get_output() loops Block enum -> calls meta.field.render(). theme/ (Colors, Icons, GitTheme) injected at display time. flags/ drives all behavior via Blocks, Layout, SortColumn, etc.
summary.change_complexity: Medium. Adding Checksum: 4 files, ~150 LOC new code. Main risk: performance (I/O on every file) and cross-platform file reading (Windows). Pattern well-established; low breakage risk if following Size/Date examples.
